what: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
text: R package downloading data Bank England Statistical Database.

what: What is the Bank of England?
title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
text: Bank England United Kingdom’s central bank. Founded 1694, responsible setting monetary policy (including Bank Rate), issuing banknotes, supervising banking system, maintaining financial stability. Monetary Policy Committee meets eight times year set interest rate ripples every mortgage, savings account, bond UK economy. Bank publishes thousands statistical time series Interactive Statistical Database - covering interest rates, exchange rates, money credit, gilt yields, housing market indicators. data underpins monetary policy analysis, financial research, economic journalism UK.

what: How is this different from existing packages?
title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
text: bbk package CRAN provides single generic function Bank England data (bbk::boe_data()), primarily Bundesbank client - Bank England one seven central banks covers, BoE support amounts raw API wrapper. still need know series codes, output requires processing. package different. built specifically Bank England provides named, documented functions series people actually use - boe_bank_rate(), boe_mortgage_rates(), boe_yield_curve(), . don’t need know Bank Rate IUDBEDR 2-year fixed mortgage rate IUMBV34. package handles series codes, date formatting, caching, error handling internally.

title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
text: data freely available, using programmatically requires knowing obscure series codes, constructing query URLs non-standard date format (DD/Mon/YYYY), parsing CSV responses irregular date formats, handling HTML error pages returned HTTP 200 status codes. Every analyst works data writes boilerplate. package replaces named functions return clean data frames.
code: # Without this package url <- paste0(   "https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/",   "_iadb-fromshowcolumns.asp?csv.x=yes",   "&SeriesCodes=IUDBEDR&UsingCodes=Y&CSVF=TN",   "&Datefrom=01/Jan/2020&Dateto=01/Jan/2025" ) raw <- read.csv(url) # ... parse dates, rename columns, handle errors ...  # With this package library(boe) boe_bank_rate(from = "2020-01-01")

title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
code: install.packages("boe")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/boe")

title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database

what: How has sterling moved against other currencies?
title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
code: # GBP/USD and GBP/EUR fx <- boe_exchange_rate(c("USD", "EUR"), from = "2024-01-01", to = "2024-01-31") head(fx, 6) #>         date currency   rate #>   2024-01-02      EUR 1.1536 #>   2024-01-03      EUR 1.1580 #>   2024-01-04      EUR 1.1591 #>   2024-01-05      EUR 1.1615 #>   2024-01-08      EUR 1.1623 #>   2024-01-09      EUR 1.1636  # See all 27 available currencies list_exchange_rates()

title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
code: # 10-year nominal gilt yield yc <- boe_yield_curve(from = "2024-01-01", to = "2024-01-31", maturity = "10yr") head(yc, 5) #>         date maturity yield_pct #>   2024-01-02     10yr    3.7190 #>   2024-01-03     10yr    3.7638 #>   2024-01-04     10yr    3.8006 #>   2024-01-05     10yr    3.8398 #>   2024-01-08     10yr    3.8619  # Full curve: 5yr, 10yr, and 20yr boe_yield_curve(from = "2024-01-01")  # Real yields boe_yield_curve(from = "2024-01-01", type = "real", measure = "zero_coupon")

what: What are mortgage rates right now?
title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
code: # All mortgage rate types mr <- boe_mortgage_rates(from = "2023-01-01")  # Latest rates (as of December 2024) #>   2yr_fixed: 4.60% #>   3yr_fixed: 4.48% #>   5yr_fixed: 4.37% #>   svr:       7.47%

what: How active is the housing market?
title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
code: # Monthly mortgage approvals - a leading indicator of housing activity ma <- boe_mortgage_approvals(from = "2019-01-01") tail(ma, 6) #>         date approvals #>   2025-08-31     64588 #>   2025-09-30     65436 #>   2025-10-31     64634 #>   2025-11-30     64018 #>   2025-12-31     61007 #>   2026-01-31     59999

title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
code: # Total consumer credit outstanding cc <- boe_consumer_credit(type = "total", from = "2024-01-01") tail(cc, 6) #>         date  type amount_gbp_m #>   2024-01-31 total       476154 #>   2024-02-29 total       479974 #>   2024-03-31 total       484269 #>   2024-04-30 total       490106 #>   2024-05-31 total       494904 #>   2024-06-30 total       498639  # Credit card debt only boe_consumer_credit(type = "credit_card", from = "2024-01-01")

what: How much money is in the economy?
title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
code: # SONIA replaced LIBOR as the UK's benchmark interest rate sonia <- boe_sonia(from = "2024-01-01", to = "2024-01-31") head(sonia, 6) #>         date rate_pct #>   2024-01-02   5.1863 #>   2024-01-03   5.1863 #>   2024-01-04   5.1870 #>   2024-01-05   5.1869 #>   2024-01-08   5.1869 #>   2024-01-09   5.1867  # Monthly or annual average boe_sonia(from = "2020-01-01", frequency = "monthly")

title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
code: # If you know the BoE series code, use boe_get() directly # Series codes: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/  # Multiple series in one call - Bank Rate vs SONIA boe_get(c("IUDBEDR", "IUDSOIA"), from = "2024-01-01", to = "2024-01-10") #>          date    code  value #>    2024-01-02 IUDBEDR 5.2500 #>    2024-01-03 IUDBEDR 5.2500 #>    2024-01-04 IUDBEDR 5.2500 #>    ... #>    2024-01-02 IUDSOIA 5.1863 #>    2024-01-03 IUDSOIA 5.1863 #>    2024-01-04 IUDSOIA 5.1870 #>    ...

title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
text: downloads cached locally user cache directory. Subsequent calls return cached copy instantly - network request made.
code: # Force a fresh download boe_bank_rate(from = "2020-01-01", cache = FALSE)  # Remove files older than 7 days clear_cache(max_age_days = 7)  # Remove all cached files clear_cache()

title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
text: package part family R packages UK economic fiscal data. share consistent interface - named functions, tidy data frames, local caching - designed work together. outputs join cleanly date columns.

title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
text: Please report bugs requests https://github.com/charlescoverdale/boe/issues.

title: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database
text: Bank England, BoE, interest rates, bank rate, SONIA, yield curve, exchange rates, mortgage rates, consumer credit, money supply, monetary policy, UK economic data, R package

what: boe: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe-package
title: boe: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe-package
text: Provides functions download tidy statistical data published Bank England https://www.bankofengland.co.uk. Covers Bank Rate, SONIA, gilt yields, exchange rates, mortgage rates, mortgage approvals, consumer credit, money supply. Series fetched Bank England Interactive Statistical Database using CSV endpoint. Data cached locally sessions.

title: boe: Download Data from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com

what: Download Bank of England Bank Rate history — boe_bank_rate
title: Download Bank of England Bank Rate history — boe_bank_rate
text: Downloads official Bank Rate (base interest rate) set Monetary Policy Committee. Available daily series January 1975.

title: Download Bank of England Bank Rate history — boe_bank_rate
code: boe_bank_rate(   from = "1975-01-02",   to = Sys.Date(),   frequency = c("daily", "monthly"),   cache = TRUE )

title: Download Bank of England Bank Rate history — boe_bank_rate
text: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/

title: Download Bank of England Bank Rate history — boe_bank_rate
text: Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults "1975-01-02". Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults today. frequency Character. One "daily" (default) "monthly" (monthly average). cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download Bank of England Bank Rate history — boe_bank_rate
text: data frame columns: date Date. Observation date. rate_pct Numeric. Bank Rate (percent).

title: Download Bank of England Bank Rate history — boe_bank_rate
Download consumer credit outstanding — boe_consumer_credit
text: Downloads monthly outstanding amounts consumer credit (total, credit cards, consumer credit). Seasonally adjusted. Available April 1993.

title: Download consumer credit outstanding — boe_consumer_credit
code: boe_consumer_credit(   type = c("total", "credit_card", "other"),   from = "1993-04-01",   to = Sys.Date(),   cache = TRUE )

title: Download consumer credit outstanding — boe_consumer_credit
text: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/

title: Download consumer credit outstanding — boe_consumer_credit
text: type Character vector. One "total", "credit_card", "". Defaults three. Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults "1993-04-01". Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults today. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download consumer credit outstanding — boe_consumer_credit
text: data frame columns: date Date. End month. type Character. Credit type. amount_gbp_m Numeric. Outstanding amount (millions pounds).

title: Download consumer credit outstanding — boe_consumer_credit
Download sterling exchange rates — boe_exchange_rate
text: Downloads daily spot exchange rates sterling major currencies Bank England. series available January 1975.

title: Download sterling exchange rates — boe_exchange_rate
code: boe_exchange_rate(   currency = "USD",   from = "1975-01-02",   to = Sys.Date(),   cache = TRUE )

title: Download sterling exchange rates — boe_exchange_rate
text: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/

title: Download sterling exchange rates — boe_exchange_rate
text: currency Character vector. One currency codes. Use list_exchange_rates() see available currencies. Defaults "USD". Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults "1975-01-02". Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults today. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download sterling exchange rates — boe_exchange_rate
text: data frame columns: date Date. Observation date. currency Character. Currency code (e.g. "USD"). rate Numeric. Units foreign currency per GBP.

title: Download sterling exchange rates — boe_exchange_rate

what: Fetch any series from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe_get
title: Fetch any series from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe_get
text: core data retrieval function. Fetches one series BoE series codes returns tidy data frame. Use convenience functions (e.g. boe_bank_rate(), boe_exchange_rate()) cover series need.

title: Fetch any series from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe_get
code: boe_get(series_codes, from = "1960-01-01", to = Sys.Date(), cache = TRUE)

title: Fetch any series from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe_get
text: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/

title: Fetch any series from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe_get
text: series_codes Character vector one BoE series codes. Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults "1960-01-01". Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults today. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Fetch any series from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe_get
text: data frame columns: date Date. Observation date. code Character. BoE series code. value Numeric. Observation value.

title: Fetch any series from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe_get
text: Series codes can found via Bank England Interactive Statistical Database https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/.

title: Fetch any series from the Bank of England Statistical Database — boe_get
Download M4 money supply — boe_money_supply
text: Downloads monthly M4 (broad money) amounts outstanding Bank England. Available June 1982.

title: Download M4 money supply — boe_money_supply
code: boe_money_supply(   from = "1982-06-01",   to = Sys.Date(),   seasonally_adjusted = TRUE,   cache = TRUE )

title: Download M4 money supply — boe_money_supply
text: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/

title: Download M4 money supply — boe_money_supply
text: Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults "1982-06-01". Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults today. seasonally_adjusted Logical. Return seasonally adjusted series (default TRUE) non-seasonally adjusted (FALSE). cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download M4 money supply — boe_money_supply
text: data frame columns: date Date. End month. amount_gbp_m Numeric. M4 amounts outstanding (millions pounds).

title: Download M4 money supply — boe_money_supply

what: Download mortgage approvals for house purchase — boe_mortgage_approvals
title: Download mortgage approvals for house purchase — boe_mortgage_approvals

title: Download mortgage approvals for house purchase — boe_mortgage_approvals
text: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/

title: Download mortgage approvals for house purchase — boe_mortgage_approvals
text: Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults "1993-04-01". Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults today. seasonally_adjusted Logical. Return seasonally adjusted series (default TRUE) non-seasonally adjusted (FALSE). cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download mortgage approvals for house purchase — boe_mortgage_approvals
text: data frame columns: date Date. End month. approvals Numeric. Number mortgage approvals.

what: Download quoted mortgage interest rates — boe_mortgage_rates
title: Download quoted mortgage interest rates — boe_mortgage_rates
text: Downloads monthly quoted (advertised) mortgage rates Bank England, including fixed-rate products standard variable rate (SVR). Available January 1995.

title: Download quoted mortgage interest rates — boe_mortgage_rates
code: boe_mortgage_rates(   type = c("2yr_fixed", "3yr_fixed", "5yr_fixed", "svr"),   from = "1995-01-01",   to = Sys.Date(),   cache = TRUE )

title: Download quoted mortgage interest rates — boe_mortgage_rates
text: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/

title: Download quoted mortgage interest rates — boe_mortgage_rates
text: type Character vector. One "2yr_fixed", "3yr_fixed", "5yr_fixed", "svr". Defaults four. Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults "1995-01-01". Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults today. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download quoted mortgage interest rates — boe_mortgage_rates
text: data frame columns: date Date. End month. type Character. Mortgage product type. rate_pct Numeric. Quoted rate (percent).

title: Download quoted mortgage interest rates — boe_mortgage_rates
Download SONIA interest rate — boe_sonia
text: Downloads Sterling Overnight Index Average (SONIA), risk-free reference rate sterling markets. Available daily January 1997.

title: Download SONIA interest rate — boe_sonia
code: boe_sonia(   from = "1997-01-02",   to = Sys.Date(),   frequency = c("daily", "monthly", "annual"),   cache = TRUE )

title: Download SONIA interest rate — boe_sonia
text: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/

title: Download SONIA interest rate — boe_sonia
text: Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults "1997-01-02". Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults today. frequency Character. One "daily" (default), "monthly" (monthly average), "annual" (annual average). cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download SONIA interest rate — boe_sonia
text: data frame columns: date Date. Observation date. rate_pct Numeric. SONIA rate (percent).

title: Download SONIA interest rate — boe_sonia
Download UK gilt yields — boe_yield_curve
text: Downloads nominal real gilt yields specified maturities Bank England yield curve data. Nominal par yields available daily late 1993; real zero-coupon yields 1985.

title: Download UK gilt yields — boe_yield_curve
code: boe_yield_curve(   from = "2000-01-01",   to = Sys.Date(),   maturity = c("5yr", "10yr", "20yr"),   type = c("nominal", "real"),   measure = c("par_yield", "zero_coupon"),   cache = TRUE )

title: Download UK gilt yields — boe_yield_curve
text: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/

title: Download UK gilt yields — boe_yield_curve
text: Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults today. maturity Character vector. One "5yr", "10yr", "20yr". Defaults three. type Character. "nominal" (default) "real". measure Character. "par_yield" (default, nominal ) "zero_coupon". cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download UK gilt yields — boe_yield_curve
text: data frame columns: date Date. Observation date. maturity Character. Maturity label (e.g. "5yr"). yield_pct Numeric. Yield (percent).

title: Download UK gilt yields — boe_yield_curve

what: Clear locally cached Bank of England data — clear_cache
title: Clear locally cached Bank of England data — clear_cache
text: Removes cached data files downloaded Bank England.

title: Clear locally cached Bank of England data — clear_cache
code: clear_cache(max_age_days = NULL)

title: Clear locally cached Bank of England data — clear_cache
text: max_age_days Numeric NULL. specified, removes files older many days. NULL (default), removes cached files.

title: Clear locally cached Bank of England data — clear_cache
text: Invisibly returns number files removed.

title: Clear locally cached Bank of England data — clear_cache
code: # \donttest{ # Remove files older than 7 days clear_cache(max_age_days = 7) #> ℹ No files older than 7 days.  # Remove everything clear_cache() #> ✔ Removed 14 cached files. # }

what: List available exchange rate currencies — list_exchange_rates
title: List available exchange rate currencies — list_exchange_rates
text: Returns data frame currency codes descriptions available Bank England exchange rate series.

title: List available exchange rate currencies — list_exchange_rates
code: list_exchange_rates()

title: List available exchange rate currencies — list_exchange_rates
text: data frame columns: currency Character. ISO currency code. description Character. Currency name. boe_code Character. BoE series code.

title: List available exchange rate currencies — list_exchange_rates
code: list_exchange_rates() #>    currency                            description boe_code #> 1       USD                              US Dollar  XUDLGBD #> 2       EUR                                   Euro  XUDLERS #> 3       JPY                           Japanese Yen  XUDLJYS #> 4       CHF                            Swiss Franc  XUDLSFS #> 5       AUD                      Australian Dollar  XUDLADS #> 6       CAD                        Canadian Dollar  XUDLCDS #> 7       NZD                     New Zealand Dollar  XUDLNDS #> 8       SEK                          Swedish Krona  XUDLSKS #> 9       NOK                        Norwegian Krone  XUDLNKS #> 10      DKK                           Danish Krone  XUDLDKS #> 11      CNY                           Chinese Yuan XUDLBK89 #> 12      INR                           Indian Rupee XUDLBK97 #> 13      HKD                       Hong Kong Dollar  XUDLHDS #> 14      SGD                       Singapore Dollar  XUDLSGS #> 15      KRW                       South Korean Won XUDLBK93 #> 16      ZAR                     South African Rand  XUDLZRS #> 17      TRY                           Turkish Lira XUDLBK95 #> 18      PLN                           Polish Zloty XUDLBK47 #> 19      CZK                           Czech Koruna XUDLBK27 #> 20      HUF                       Hungarian Forint XUDLBK35 #> 21      ILS                         Israeli Shekel XUDLBK65 #> 22      SAR                            Saudi Riyal  XUDLSRS #> 23      THB                              Thai Baht XUDLBK87 #> 24      TWD                          Taiwan Dollar  XUDLTWS #> 25      BRL                         Brazilian Real XUDLB8KL #> 26      MYR                      Malaysian Ringgit XUDLBK83 #> 27      ERI Sterling Effective Exchange Rate Index XUDLBK67

title: boe 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-12 Initial release. boe_get(): fetch series code BoE Statistical Database. boe_bank_rate(): Bank Rate history (daily monthly, 1975). boe_sonia(): SONIA interest rate (daily, monthly, annual, 1997). boe_yield_curve(): nominal real gilt yields 5yr, 10yr, 20yr maturities (1985/1993). boe_exchange_rate(): daily sterling exchange rates 27 currencies (1975). list_exchange_rates(): catalogue available currency codes. boe_mortgage_rates(): quoted mortgage rates (2yr/3yr/5yr fixed, SVR, 1995). boe_mortgage_approvals(): monthly mortgage approvals house purchase (1993). boe_consumer_credit(): consumer credit outstanding type (1993). boe_money_supply(): M4 broad money amounts outstanding (1982). clear_cache(): delete locally cached data files.
